events.title: Extremist Mob Forces Congregation from New Meeting Place in Indonesia
events.body:
A Muslim mob led by members of the Islamic Jihad Front (FJI) forcibly halted a church service in Indonesia on May 24, according to Morning Star News. More than a dozen masked and helmeted FJI members, joined by local residents, pushed their way into the congregation's newly rented building just before 8 a.m. as worshippers were preparing for the service. The mob chanted, shouted threats, and shoved church leaders while police stood by. Frightened congregants, including children and elderly members, left the premises by 8:30 a.m.

The congregation had recently moved to the new location after rising costs made their previous meeting place unaffordable. The mob objected that the church had not yet obtained a worship permit for the new site and argued its presence in a Muslim-majority area threatened social harmony. One voice in a video of the incident threatened to burn the building down if police withdrew. A church spokesperson told Morning Star News that the intimidation traumatized the congregation, particularly the children, and constituted a violation of Indonesia's constitutional guarantees of religious freedom under its Pancasila framework.

Prayer: for traumatized believers in Indonesia and the legal protection of their right to worship freely ---
events.prayer: for traumatized believers in Indonesia and the legal protection of their right to worship freely ---

events.title: House Church Preacher and Two Believers Detained in China on "Illegal Gathering" Charges
events.body:
Chinese authorities raided a small house church during a Sunday worship service in April, detaining a preacher and two other believers on charges of "organizing illegal gatherings," according to ChinaAid. More than a month later, formal arrest notices were delivered to the families of all three men, who remain in detention. The congregation has around 30 members, many of whom suffer from depression or cancer, and describe the church as both a place of worship and a source of emotional and spiritual support.

The detained preacher is reported to be in critical health, suffering from multiple serious conditions including liver damage, kidney insufficiency, and severe vascular disease that cannot be treated surgically. His wife, who also lives with chronic illness, is said to be under significant psychological and financial strain. According to ChinaAid, the arrests reflect a broader national crackdown on unregistered churches that has intensified since revised Regulations on Religious Affairs took effect in 2018, generating fear and a chilling effect among independent religious communities across China.

The congregation is notably small and serves largely marginalized individuals, including those struggling with illness and addiction. Despite the legal risks, the church had continued to meet for worship prior to the raid.

Prayer: for the three detained believers and the vulnerable congregation left behind, especially the critically ill preacher facing deteriorating health in custody
events.prayer: for the three detained believers and the vulnerable congregation left behind, especially the critically ill preacher facing deteriorating health in custody

events.title: Twenty Christians Remain Hostage After Pastors Killed Returning from Peace Conference in India
events.body:
Hours after gunmen killed three pastors returning from a peace conference on May 13, armed groups began kidnapping Christians in Manipur, India. According to International Christian Concern, 20 believers remain held hostage as of late May 2026. The three pastors killed had just attended a Baptist assembly focused on reconciliation and peacebuilding among the region's tribal communities.

The abductions began when armed groups from one tribal community kidnapped civilians from a rival tribe, prompting retaliatory abductions in return. Negotiations led by church leaders, tribal leaders, civil society groups, and security agencies resulted in the release of 28 hostages — 14 from each side — on May 16, following a day-long standoff the previous day. The wives of the slain pastors were reported by The Indian Express to be among those released. Today, 14 hostages from one community and six from the other remain in captivity, with a lack of information about the latter group fueling fears they may have been killed.

International advocacy groups CSW and 21Wilberforce have both issued public appeals calling for the immediate release of all remaining hostages, a transparent investigation into the killings, and an urgent renewal of peacebuilding efforts among the tribal Christian communities of northeast India.

Prayer: for the 20 believers still in captivity and the families of the three pastors killed
events.prayer: for the 20 believers still in captivity and the families of the three pastors killed

events.title: Over 180 Tribal Christian Families Denied Water and Livelihoods in Faith-Based Boycott
events.body:
More than 180 Christian families across dozens of villages in India have reportedly been denied access to communal water sources and basic livelihood opportunities for the past three weeks, according to International Christian Concern (ICC). The families, belonging to indigenous Adivasi communities, have been barred from using rivers, ponds, taps, and hand pumps, while also being blocked from government employment schemes and the collection of forest produce essential to their survival.

ICC reports that around 26 families have been denied water access, nearly 41 families have been excluded from a national rural employment program, and more than 100 families have been barred from Tendu leaf procurement — a significant source of income in the region. Firewood belonging to four families was also reportedly seized by force. Community leaders state that the boycotts are deliberately intended to pressure believers into abandoning Christianity and participating in *Ghar Wapsi* — formal reconversion to Hinduism. With the rainy agricultural season approaching, affected families now fear they will also be prevented from cultivating their lands and harvesting crops.

ICC notes that the region has a history of anti-Christian tensions, with local village authorities previously passing resolutions restricting Christian burials and pastoral visits, and that several past situations have ended in violence.

Prayer: for the Adivasi Christian families facing economic strangulation and pressure to renounce their faith
events.prayer: for the Adivasi Christian families facing economic strangulation and pressure to renounce their faith

events.title: 31 House Church Members Sentenced in China's Largest Recent Christian Prosecution
events.body:
In one of the largest coordinated prosecutions of Christians in recent years, 31 members of a house church in China have been sentenced to years-long prison terms tied directly to their religious activities, according to ChinaAid. Arrests began in March 2024 when leaders of the fellowship were detained in a major crackdown targeting itinerant church leaders, with a formal prosecution hearing held for eight members in November 2024.

According to ChinaAid, authorities subsequently fragmented the case into numerous smaller proceedings involving one or two defendants at a time, in an apparent effort to limit public attention and international scrutiny. Sentences among the 31 believers range from two years and four months to four years in prison. An elderly pastor faces three years and two months alongside a financial penalty. Authorities reportedly relied on surveillance, coerced testimony, confiscated religious materials, and online communications, with court documents citing Bible study meetings and prayer gatherings as evidence of wrongdoing. The prosecutions invoke Article 300 of China's Criminal Law, a measure frequently used against unregistered Christian groups designated as cult organizations by the state.

ChinaAid also reports that more than a dozen house church leaders were secretly sentenced to years-long terms in a related case in another region roughly two years ago, suggesting a broader nationwide pattern of targeting independent Christian communities that operate outside state-controlled religious institutions.

Prayer: for the 31 imprisoned believers and their families, and all house church communities facing state repression in China
events.prayer: for the 31 imprisoned believers and their families, and all house church communities facing state repression in China

events.title: Myanmar Military Seizes Border Towns from Predominantly Christian Ethnic Minorities
events.body:
Myanmar's military has seized two strategic border towns from ethnic minority militias, tightening its grip on communities that are predominantly Christian. According to International Christian Concern, one town on the border with Thailand was taken from Karen forces, while another near the border with India had been held by Chin fighters since May 2024. The military now controls nearly 90% of parliamentary seats following an election widely condemned as rigged, with main opposition parties excluded from participating.

The Chin and Karen peoples are among Myanmar's most populous ethnic minorities and contain unusually high concentrations of Christians — up to 90% of the Chin and around 50% of the Karen identify as Christian — making them targets of the military's violent Buddhist nationalism. The military's ranks have grown significantly through forced conscription, and China and Russia have supplied it with arms. Investigative outlet Bellingcat has documented how the military systematically destroys villages, even erasing them from maps after bombing and burning them. Analysts report growing war-weariness among resistance communities, raising fears that decades-long ethnic armed resistance could collapse entirely.

Prayer: for myanmar's Christian ethnic minorities facing military advance and the collapse of armed resistance
events.prayer: for myanmar's Christian ethnic minorities facing military advance and the collapse of armed resistance

events.title: Church Razed in Under 72 Hours as Chinese Authorities Deploy Surveillance Blockade
events.body:
A prominent church in eastern China was demolished in less than 72 hours by local authorities and Chinese Communist Party officials using sweeping security measures and advanced surveillance technology, according to ChinaAid. The operation began on May 17, when officials moved into the building, with demolition commencing the following morning. Heavy construction vehicles subsequently removed all rubble in an apparent effort to eliminate any visible trace of the structure.

According to a local source interviewed by ChinaAid, authorities established barricades and checkpoints up to two kilometers from the site, forcibly relocated nearby residents, and stationed monitoring personnel in surrounding high-rise buildings days before the demolition. Thermal infrared imaging equipment was reportedly deployed across the area. An information blockade was also enforced: anyone seen with a mobile phone near the site risked immediate detention and device inspection. Church members have since grown afraid to send even messages or images in private online group chats, fearing real-time backend surveillance could lead to them being identified and summoned by authorities. The incident followed months of escalating tensions between the congregation and local officials.

Prayer: for believers living under intensifying state surveillance and the silencing of their witness
events.prayer: for believers living under intensifying state surveillance and the silencing of their witness

events.title: Three Christians Released on Bail in China After Charges Over Religious Publishing
events.body:
Three Christians have been released on bail pending trial in a case involving the production, sale, and distribution of Christian books and religious materials, according to ChinaAid. The three church members were charged with "illegal business operations" by Chinese authorities. All three were granted bail on May 20, following preliminary hearings and a prosecutorial review, in a decision that surprised family members and supporters who had been praying for the defendants.

According to the defense lawyer, prosecutors concluded that the evidence did not sufficiently establish a profit-making motive. Supporters had previously circulated a statement from a foreign national who identified himself as the project's primary organizer, accepted responsibility for the publishing effort, and said the three believers were not principal decision-makers. The materials were described as focused solely on faith, scholarship, and Christian apologetics, with no political content. ChinaAid notes that Chinese authorities increasingly deploy economic-crime charges against unregistered religious groups and independent publishers. Under Chinese legal procedures, the case could be dismissed after one year if police fail to submit additional evidence, though it remains uncertain whether the investigation will be expanded to include others connected to the project.

Prayer: for the three believers on bail and all Christians in China facing prosecution for publishing or distributing Scripture
events.prayer: for the three believers on bail and all Christians in China facing prosecution for publishing or distributing Scripture

events.title: House Church Preacher and Two Believers Detained in China on "Illegal Gathering" Charges
events.body:
Chinese authorities raided a small house church during a Sunday worship service in April, detaining a preacher and two other believers on charges of "organizing illegal gatherings," according to ChinaAid. More than a month later, formal arrest notices were delivered to the families of all three men, who remain in detention at a local facility.

The congregation, which numbers just over 30 members, serves primarily marginalized individuals and those living with serious illnesses including depression and cancer. The detained preacher, who himself suffers from multiple severe conditions including liver damage, kidney insufficiency, and inoperable varicose veins, was reportedly involved in outreach to drug users before his arrest. His wife, who also has chronic health conditions, is said to be under significant psychological and financial strain. ChinaAid reports that authorities have also repeatedly summoned and questioned other church members, creating a broader climate of fear among independent religious communities in the area.

The arrests reflect a pattern documented since China's revised Regulations on Religious Affairs took effect in 2018, under which unregistered house churches across the country have faced raids, fines, and the detention of leaders. The "illegal gathering" charge has become an increasingly common tool used against congregations that operate outside state-sanctioned religious structures.

Prayer: for the detained preacher's deteriorating health and the wellbeing of his chronically ill wife and congregation
events.prayer: for the detained preacher's deteriorating health and the wellbeing of his chronically ill wife and congregation

events.title: Pakistani Christian Girls Face Surge in Forced Conversion Through Abduction and Marriage
events.body:
Pakistan is experiencing a troubling rise in the abduction of underage girls from religious minority communities, who are then forcibly converted to Islam and married to Muslim men. According to International Christian Concern, multiple reports estimate that approximately 1,000 such abductions occur annually, though a Pakistani Christian source told ICC the real figure is significantly higher, as most families never file a report — often because they are threatened into silence or believe legal action will be fruitless. Most victims are Hindu or Christian girls.

One documented case involved a 12-year-old Christian girl abducted during a home invasion, who was subsequently raped and shackled following her forced conversion and marriage. A more recent high-profile case involved a 13-year-old Christian girl taken from her home, converted, and married to a man in his forties. In March 2026, Pakistan's Federal Constitutional Court ruled her conversion valid and ordered her returned to her husband. A Pakistani Christian source told ICC that judicial rulings consistently favour the husband and that all levels of authority are effectively stacked against non-Muslims. U.N. experts have also raised concerns about the impunity with which perpetrators operate.

Abductors use varied methods to target victims, including approaching girls online through social media platforms and posing as wealthy potential friends. The girls targeted typically come from impoverished backgrounds, making them especially vulnerable to manipulation and deception.

Prayer: for pakistani Christian girls at risk of abduction and forced conversion, and the families threatened into silence
events.prayer: for pakistani Christian girls at risk of abduction and forced conversion, and the families threatened into silence

events.title: Fulani Christians in Nigeria's Middle Belt Denounce Violence and Seek Recognition Amid Rising Threats
events.body:
Fulani Christian leaders and converts in Nigeria's Middle Belt are calling for greater recognition of peaceful Fulani communities, according to International Christian Concern (ICC). They say they are increasingly trapped between attacks by armed militias and growing public suspicion tied to their ethnic identity. A leader of a Fulani Christian outreach ministry told ICC that his life has been threatened because of his public statements distancing Fulani Christians from armed groups, including a cattle breeders association that the U.S. government has labeled a terror group and which is linked to killings of Christian farmers.

The Fulani, one of Africa's largest ethnic groups, have faced years of killings, kidnappings, and village raids in Nigeria's Middle Belt, fueling tensions between farming communities and armed militias. Peaceful Fulani families, including Christians, say they too have suffered from this violence. The ministry referenced by ICC has more than 5,000 members engaged in evangelism across Nigeria and parts of West Africa, and has recorded more than 1,000 converts. It also provides humanitarian support including food, clothing, and Christian literature in multiple languages to pastoral communities, seeking to demonstrate that Fulani identity and Christian faith are not in conflict.

Prayer: for fulani Christian leaders facing death threats and the communities caught between militia violence and ethnic suspicion
events.prayer: for fulani Christian leaders facing death threats and the communities caught between militia violence and ethnic suspicion

events.title: Gunmen Kill Three and Abduct Fifteen at Church Night Vigil in Western Nigeria
events.body:
Gunmen attacked a church prayer vigil in western Nigeria on the night of May 23, killing three Christians and abducting 15 others, according to Morning Star News. The assault took place at approximately 8:30 p.m., when armed men invaded the open-air prayer ground, opened fire indiscriminately, and fled with the abducted worshippers to an unknown destination.

Regional authorities condemned the attack as "barbaric, callous and unacceptable" and launched a coordinated rescue operation involving drone units, intelligence assets, and paramilitary personnel. Local government officials subsequently directed all churches in the area to suspend night vigil services until further notice, citing ongoing terrorist activity in the region. A local human rights organization also condemned the assault, warning that continued attacks on civilians were creating widespread fear and community displacement.

The attack reflects a broader and worsening pattern of violence targeting Christian gatherings in Nigeria's middle-belt and western regions. Authorities have repeatedly urged faith communities in isolated areas to avoid holding nighttime worship services, highlighting the dangerous environment facing Nigerian Christians who seek to practice their faith openly.

Prayer: for the families of those killed, the 15 abducted worshippers, and the endangered Christian communities of western Nigeria
events.prayer: for the families of those killed, the 15 abducted worshippers, and the endangered Christian communities of western Nigeria

events.title: Arrests Made in Murder of Catholic Environmental Activist Killed After Exposing Alleged Mining Corruption
events.body:
Honduran authorities have arrested a former local official and two others accused of masterminding the murder of a Catholic environmental activist who was killed in September 2024. The former official was arrested in mid-May and placed in preventive detention, with a preliminary hearing scheduled for later this year. According to Crux Now, the activist had been shot by hitmen after leaving a Catholic community celebration.

The slain activist had been a vocal critic of alleged fraudulent mining licenses issued within a protected national park, and had formally reported the matter to prosecutors at a Catholic parish he regularly attended. His killing came years after he first raised those concerns. Catholics who knew him have welcomed the arrests but are calling for a broader investigation, with a Church-affiliated regional network telling Crux Now that three distinct criminal structures — involving corrupt officials, contract killers, and business interests — must all be dismantled. Political connections reportedly delayed the investigation for over a year.

Prayer: for full justice for the murdered Catholic activist and protection for those continuing to expose corruption in Honduras
events.prayer: for full justice for the murdered Catholic activist and protection for those continuing to expose corruption in Honduras

events.title: Two Muslim-Background Converts Released After Nearly Two Years Imprisoned for Their Faith in Uganda
events.body:
Two Christian converts in Uganda have been released on bail after spending nearly two years in prison following their decision to leave Islam and embrace Christianity, according to International Christian Concern. The men publicly accepted Jesus Christ in early 2024 after attending teachings at an Anglican church, but their conversion sparked immediate hostility from local Muslims. Shortly after declaring their new faith, they were arrested at the church premises on accusations of stealing mosque mats and a Quran.

Local Christians maintained that the charges lacked credible evidence and were fabricated to punish the men for abandoning Islam. During their imprisonment, Muslim leaders reportedly pressured the converts to renounce Christianity in exchange for freedom, but both men refused. Church leaders continued to minister to them behind bars, and both converts said their faith grew stronger through the ordeal. In April 2026, a legal hearing arranged through church intervention exposed contradictions in the original accusations, leading to their release. A church bishop condemned what he described as attempts to criminalize genuine conversion to Christianity.

The two men remain on bail and face an uncertain path forward, but both say their faith in Jesus is unshaken. Their case reflects a broader pattern of converts from Islam facing social and legal pressure in parts of Uganda.

Prayer: for the two released converts and all former Muslims facing legal or communal pressure for their faith in Uganda
events.prayer: for the two released converts and all former Muslims facing legal or communal pressure for their faith in Uganda

events.title: Survivors of Pakistan Church Bombing Excluded from Compensation Thirteen Years On
events.body:
Thirteen years after twin suicide blasts killed nearly 100 people and wounded more than 200 others at a church in Pakistan, survivors are still fighting for justice. According to International Christian Concern, the attack — claimed by Tehrik-e-Taliban Pakistan — stands as one of the deadliest assaults on Christians in the country's history. In May 2026, the provincial government finally held a ceremony to distribute the first 37 compensation checks, providing funds to widows, orphans, and people with disabilities from the 2013 attack.

Despite this partial step forward, multiple severely injured survivors attended the distribution ceremony only to be turned away empty-handed. Authorities ruled them ineligible on the grounds that they had "fully recovered" from their wounds — a justification activists say ignores the reality that many lost their livelihoods and accumulated significant debt paying for medical treatment. The compensation fund itself has also been repeatedly diverted: originally established as a dedicated relief fund for blast victims, it was converted into a general minority welfare fund covering all religious minorities in the province, diluting the aid intended specifically for those affected by the bombing.

Prayer: for blast survivors denied compensation and the long-deferred pursuit of justice for Pakistan's Christian community
events.prayer: for blast survivors denied compensation and the long-deferred pursuit of justice for Pakistan's Christian community

events.title: ADF Kills Ten Christians in Overnight Raid, Expanding Terror Into New DRC Province
events.body:
Ten Christians were killed and others kidnapped during an overnight assault by the Allied Democratic Forces (ADF) on a village in the DRC's Haut-Uele province on April 30, according to International Christian Concern. Attackers struck while the community was asleep, forcing residents from their homes, killing people, and dragging others into the bush. Survivors fled in panic as the violence unfolded in the darkness.

The attack marks a significant geographic expansion of ADF activity. The group had previously concentrated its atrocities in North Kivu, Ituri, and surrounding territories, but this assault signals its reach into new regions of the country. Civil society leaders warned that insecurity across eastern DRC is intensifying and urged communities to remain vigilant. Military authorities confirmed the attack and stated that security forces had regained control of the area, with national army operations continuing. Provincial officials encouraged displaced residents to return home, saying the situation had stabilized.

Prayer: for the families of the Christians killed and kidnapped, and an end to ADF expansion into new regions of eastern DRC
events.prayer: for the families of the Christians killed and kidnapped, and an end to ADF expansion into new regions of eastern DRC

events.title: ADF Attack Kills at Least 17 Christians as Ebola Outbreak Deepens Crisis in Eastern DR Congo
events.body:
At least 17 Christians were killed when Allied Democratic Forces (ADF) militants attacked a community in eastern DR Congo on May 19, according to International Christian Concern. The assault began in one village before spreading to a nearby settlement, where several homes were burned. Local civil society leaders warn the death toll is provisional, as many people remain missing and ongoing insecurity has made it difficult to confirm the full extent of casualties.

The attackers reportedly split into two groups after the assault, moving in opposite directions through surrounding forested areas, raising fears of further violence. Local leaders are urging residents to stay alert and report suspicious activity. The attack compounds an already dire situation: an active Ebola outbreak in the same region has placed strict health requirements on the population — precautions that become impossible for those fleeing gunfire. Displaced families hiding in forests have no access to clean water or medical care, creating conditions that accelerate both the spread of disease and the reach of the ADF. Leaders warn that without sustained military intervention, militants could entrench further into remote areas, leaving civilians trapped between armed attacks and a deadly epidemic.

Prayer: for the bereaved Christian communities caught between ADF violence and the Ebola outbreak
events.prayer: for the bereaved Christian communities caught between ADF violence and the Ebola outbreak

events.title: Six House Church Leaders Arrested in China Over Sunday School Classes and Tithing
events.body:
Six Christians — five men and one woman — have been arrested in China and charged with fraud and "organizing the activities of minors that undermine public order," according to ChinaAid. All six are leaders of an unregistered house church. They remain in detention while their families and supporters advocate on their behalf.

According to ChinaAid, the fraud charges are being used to penalize the church for receiving tithes and offerings, while the "activities of minors" charge stems directly from the church organizing Sunday school classes for children. Officials carried out the detention without first receiving legal opinions from the defendants' attorneys, a procedural violation. ChinaAid notes that China's Public Security Administration Punishments Law, updated last year, now authorizes authorities to criminally punish unregistered religious activities. China ranks 17th on Open Doors' World Watch List of the most dangerous countries to be a Christian.

ChinaAid is calling on the international community, democratic governments, and human rights organizations to monitor the case closely, describing the charges and legal process as representing a serious escalation in China's suppression of independent religious belief.

Prayer: for the six detained house church leaders and their families awaiting justice
events.prayer: for the six detained house church leaders and their families awaiting justice

events.title: Pakistan Police Defy Court Orders in 2023 Anti-Christian Mob Attack Cases
events.body:
Pakistani police have failed to comply with Supreme Court orders to arrest suspects involved in the August 2023 mob attacks on multiple churches and Christian homes, according to Morning Star News. Despite a March 31 Supreme Court ruling directing police to arrest absconding suspects and ordering an anti-terrorism court to conclude trials within six months, a church leader says authorities have made little meaningful progress.

Of 336 suspects against whom charge sheets have been filed, only one remains in prison; the rest have secured bail or been discharged, according to Morning Star News. Police and intelligence reports estimated that more than 5,000 people participated in the attacks, and while nearly 400 suspects were initially arrested, most were later released due to weak investigations and insufficient evidence. Some economically vulnerable Christian complainants also faced intimidation and gave statements favorable to the accused. A church leader called for three First Information Reports placed into closed-file status to be reopened, noting that the state has both the authority and resources to prosecute those named.

Pakistan's Senate Standing Committee on Human Rights rejected a police report on the investigation as unsatisfactory and summoned the Inspector General of Punjab Police to appear before lawmakers with a revised report.

Prayer: for pakistani Christians awaiting justice and protection from ongoing intimidation by suspects
events.prayer: for pakistani Christians awaiting justice and protection from ongoing intimidation by suspects

events.title: Unregistered Church Demolished in Eastern China as Crackdown Intensifies
events.body:
Authorities in eastern China have demolished a prominent unregistered Protestant church in a region historically known as "China's Jerusalem," razing its multi-level sanctuary using large excavators, according to ChinaAid. The exact demolition date remains unknown due to an information blackout, though sources provided an initial report on May 19, 2026. Ahead of the demolition, authorities covered the church cross with black cloth and established security checkpoints approximately one kilometer from the site to prevent public access.

The crackdown on this congregation began late last year when authorities arrested over 100 church members in a pre-dawn raid and seized control of the building, as confirmed by reporting from Le Monde. Four additional members were arrested concurrently with the demolition, joining 18 others already jailed. Families of all detained individuals reportedly received official warnings to remain silent. According to ChinaAid, the broader suppression campaign in the surrounding area has included continuous surveillance, strict information controls, and the closure of businesses linked to alleged church members. A ChinaAid representative stated the persecution of Christians by Chinese authorities has become increasingly institutionalized and targeted.

Prayer: for may God sustain the detained believers and their silenced families with courage and peace, and may the global Church bear witness to their suffering.
events.prayer: for may God sustain the detained believers and their silenced families with courage and peace, and may the global Church bear witness to their suffering.

events.title: Three Pastors Killed in Ambush While Returning from Peace Conference
events.body:
Three pastors were killed and four others injured in an armed ambush on May 13 in India, according to International Christian Concern. The church leaders were returning from a peace and reconciliation conference when approximately ten armed men opened fire on two vehicles using automatic weapons. The attack took place amid ongoing ethnic tensions in the region involving multiple tribal communities.

The killings triggered immediate retaliatory hostage-taking by rival armed factions, with as many as 38 civilians — including women and religious figures — abducted and moved to undisclosed locations. Government officials, security forces, and church leaders launched emergency negotiations, resulting in the release of approximately 30 hostages by May 15. However, civil society groups reported that some individuals remained in captivity and were feared dead. The ambush occurred against a backdrop of ethnic conflict that has simmered since 2023, rooted in disputes over land rights, tribal status, and demographic concerns, which a human rights activist described as now escalating into a "dangerous triangular confrontation."

One of the slain pastors had been an active peacemaker, having recently led efforts to broker reconciliation between rival communities. Regional government leaders condemned the killings and pledged full state resources to pursue justice.

Prayer: for may God bring lasting peace to the tribal communities of this region, protect those still in captivity, and honor the sacrifice of these pastors who died while pursuing reconciliation.
events.prayer: for may God bring lasting peace to the tribal communities of this region, protect those still in captivity, and honor the sacrifice of these pastors who died while pursuing reconciliation.

events.title: Christian Teacher Beheaded Following Coordinated School Kidnappings in Nigeria
events.body:
A Christian math teacher was beheaded following coordinated attacks on three schools in Nigeria, according to International Christian Concern. Armed men arrived on motorcycles and stormed the schools on May 15, kidnapping seven teachers and several pupils before fleeing into nearby forests. Days later, a video circulated online appearing to show the teacher being beheaded.

The killings prompted community protests, with teachers, youth, and residents blocking major roads and demanding stronger protection for schools and rural communities. Church leaders and civil society groups called for the release of remaining hostages and the arrest of those responsible. Security agencies had not publicly confirmed the full number of those still missing at the time of reporting.

According to International Christian Concern, kidnappings for ransom have become increasingly frequent in parts of Southwest Nigeria in recent years. A pastor speaking at a local prayer gathering urged authorities to strengthen intelligence operations and better protect vulnerable communities. Religious leaders and residents continued to appeal to federal and state authorities to secure schools and bring the perpetrators to justice.

Prayer: for may God bring swift justice for the slain teacher, grant safe return to all remaining hostages, and give Nigerian authorities the resolve to protect Christian communities and schools.
events.prayer: for may God bring swift justice for the slain teacher, grant safe return to all remaining hostages, and give Nigerian authorities the resolve to protect Christian communities and schools.

events.title: Azerbaijan Demolishes Armenian Churches in Seized Region, Raising Cultural Genocide Fears
events.body:
Satellite imagery has confirmed the demolition of two Armenian churches in the disputed Nagorno-Karabakh region, known to Armenians as Artsakh, according to International Christian Concern and Radio Free Europe. Images taken in late April show that both structures were razed in recent weeks after surviving years of conflict. One of the demolished churches had been consecrated as recently as 2019 and previously served as a civilian bomb shelter during wartime. A government-affiliated Azerbaijani religious body confirmed the demolitions, characterizing the churches as illegitimate structures.

The destruction comes less than three years after Azerbaijan seized full control of Nagorno-Karabakh in September 2023, prompting the exodus of approximately 120,000 ethnic Armenians from the region. Armenian church leaders have accused Azerbaijan of deliberately targeting Christian holy sites to erase Armenian history from the territory. Research supported by satellite imagery has confirmed damage or destruction to hundreds of religious and cultural heritage sites across Artsakh. International observers remain largely barred from the region, forcing journalists and researchers to rely on satellite analysis. Some advocacy organizations have described the pattern of destruction as cultural genocide, noting that the erasure of churches is inseparable from the forced removal of the Christian population that once worshipped there. PRAYER: Lord, intervene to halt the destruction of Armenian Christian heritage and bring justice and protection to a displaced and grieving people.

events.title: Bomb Blast Strikes Catholic Churchyard During Sunday Mass in Papua
events.body:
A bomb or grenade detonated in the churchyard of a Catholic parish in Papua, Indonesia, on Sunday, May 17, around 12:15 p.m., just as the closing prayer of a Mass was beginning. Four people sustained serious injuries in the blast, according to International Christian Concern. A local priest confirmed that only two of the four wounded could initially be transported to hospital, with the remaining two transferred the following day.

According to International Christian Concern, this attack is part of a decades-long pattern of violence in Papua, where armed conflict between Indonesian national security forces and a separatist movement has persisted since the 1960s. A leader of the Papuan People's Council warned that tens to hundreds of people have already been killed or injured this year alone, with communities fleeing their homes and churches, children dropping out of school, and health services collapsing across the region. He called on Indonesian military forces to act more professionally, properly identify their targets, and refrain from indiscriminate use of bombs or gunfire — particularly in civilian and religious spaces. PRAYER: May God bring protection to Papuan Christian communities caught in the crossfire of armed conflict, and grant wisdom to those in authority to distinguish between combatants and civilians.

events.title: Christian Girl with Disabilities Allegedly Abducted, Forcibly Converted, and Married in Pakistan
events.body:
A married Muslim man abducted a 14-year-old Christian girl on May 12, forcibly converted her to Islam, and married her, according to Morning Star News. The girl, who works as a domestic helper, disappeared from her employer's household. CCTV footage showed her leaving with an unidentified man, prompting police to register an abduction report. The suspect was later identified after the girl's father circulated a screenshot from the footage among neighborhood residents.

The girl suffers from epilepsy and mental health challenges, which her father told Morning Star News made her "an easy target." The suspect is reportedly 41 years old, had been married multiple times, and allegedly disappeared the same day as the abduction, taking children from a previous marriage with him. Authorities later presented documents claiming the girl had converted to Islam and married the suspect months before the abduction — documents the family's attorney believes were fabricated. According to Morning Star News, at least two of the suspect's other wives are now cooperating with the legal investigation after learning the full extent of his conduct. The father has not disclosed the crime to extended family, fearing it will harm the marriage prospects of his younger daughters. PRAYER: May God rescue this vulnerable Christian girl, bring her safely home, and grant justice through Pakistan's courts.

events.title: Two House Church Members Placed in Secretive Chinese Detention System Amid Ongoing Crackdown
events.body:
Chinese authorities have placed two members of a prominent house church under "Residential Surveillance at a Designated Location" (RSDL), a secretive detention system criticized by human rights groups and U.N. experts as a form of enforced disappearance, according to ChinaAid. The two believers — a deputy deacon and a fellow church member — were detained on January 6, 2026, as part of a broader crackdown targeting the congregation.

The house church involved has been under intense government scrutiny since a sweeping raid in December 2018, when its pastor was ultimately sentenced to nine years in prison on charges of "inciting subversion of state power." The deputy deacon detained in January had previously faced years of harassment, including evictions and utility shutoffs, yet continued leading Bible studies and supporting fellow believers. Rights advocates warn that those held under RSDL are routinely denied access to lawyers and family members, and may be subjected to prolonged interrogation, sleep deprivation, and psychological pressure. Families of the two detainees have reportedly received no formal legal documentation regarding their loved ones' whereabouts. PRAYER: Lord, sustain and protect these two detained believers in body and spirit, and may their families receive news of their safety and swift release.

events.title: Trump Raises Cases of Imprisoned Pastor and Catholic Journalist in Beijing Summit with Xi
events.body:
During a recent summit in Beijing, U.S. President Trump raised the cases of two prominent religious prisoners with Chinese President Xi Jinping, according to International Christian Concern. Trump confirmed aboard Air Force One on the return journey that he had advocated for both men. Xi reportedly pledged to "strongly consider" the release of one imprisoned pastor — described by Trump as a rare public acknowledgment from Beijing amid China's intensifying crackdown on independent Christianity.

The pastor in question founded one of China's largest unregistered Protestant congregations, which authorities shut down in 2018 for refusing to register with the state-controlled church body. He was detained last year alongside nearly 30 church leaders and members, accused of illegally distributing religious teachings online under tightened regulations. The second prisoner, a devout Catholic and pro-democracy media figure, is serving a 20-year sentence under Hong Kong's national security law. His family reports that authorities are blocking him from practicing his faith, including receiving communion. Trump acknowledged the second case would be "a tougher one." Members of Congress had introduced resolutions ahead of the summit calling for both men's release. PRAYER: May God sovereignly move in the hearts of Chinese authorities to release these imprisoned believers and restore freedom of worship across China.

events.title: Christian Publishing Couple Suffers Deteriorating Health and Abuse in Chinese Prisons
events.body:
A married couple who operated a Christian publishing business in China continue to suffer severe mistreatment in separate prisons, according to International Christian Concern. The two were involved in printing Christian literature beginning in 2015, until state security police raided and shut down their company in July 2020, seizing more than 210,000 Christian books. They were arrested that same month and sentenced in 2021 on charges of printing materials without proper government licenses.

Both prisoners have endured torture, starvation, and abuse from guards and fellow inmates, and their health has deteriorated rapidly, according to their son. The husband has developed a serious spinal condition and is struggling to eat. His wife is experiencing severe headaches, dizziness, and ongoing physical abuse from prison guards. International Christian Concern reports that conditions in their respective facilities remain brutal with no signs of improvement. PRAYER: Lord, bring urgent physical healing and relief to this imprisoned couple, and move authorities to release them from unjust captivity.

events.title: Finnish Parliamentarian Appeals Hate Speech Conviction Over Christian Pamphlet to European Court
events.body:
A Finnish member of Parliament announced on May 7 that she is appealing the Finnish Supreme Court's ruling against her to the European Court of Human Rights, according to International Christian Concern. The court's March decision, reached by a 3-2 majority, acquitted her for a 2019 Bible verse shared on social media but convicted her of hate speech for a Christian pamphlet she published in 2004. The court ordered her to pay a 1,800-euro fine, equivalent to 20 days' pay.

The conviction relied on a section of Finland's Criminal Code concerning war crimes and crimes against humanity — a law that was not introduced until 2008, four years after the pamphlet was published. The parliamentarian, speaking through ADF International, warned that the ruling sets a dangerous precedent for freedom of speech across Europe. Her case has drawn international attention, including an appearance before a U.S. House Judiciary Committee hearing on free speech in February. She stated that she hopes the European Court of Human Rights will affirm that peacefully expressing one's beliefs is never a criminal offense. PRAYER: May the European Court of Human Rights uphold the freedom of Christians to express their faith peacefully, and may this case protect believers across Europe from unjust prosecution under hate speech laws.

events.title: Indonesian Congregation Denied Church Construction Permit for Over Two Decades
events.body:
A Christian congregation in Indonesia has been blocked from building a permanent place of worship for over 22 years, according to International Christian Concern. Efforts to construct the church began in 2004 with community support and a groundbreaking ceremony attended by leaders from multiple faiths. However, within months the partially built structure was vandalized and burned, and no suspects were ever identified by authorities.

Subsequent attempts to secure building permits were repeatedly obstructed — local officials refused to sign required support documents, residents protested and blocked access to the site in 2014, and the congregation was forced to relocate its worship services to another location. In 2025, the committee again submitted a proposal claiming it had met all legal requirements for community support. However, a letter of rejection allegedly signed by residents — with questions raised about the involvement of a local official in gathering those signatures — was delivered months late in early 2026, further complicating the process.

Local authorities are currently reported to be verifying the community support data. International Christian Concern notes that if the legal requirements are found to have been met, there is no lawful basis to deny the construction permit. The case has drawn significant public attention amid hopes for a just resolution. PRAYER: May Indonesian authorities act with justice and transparency, granting this congregation the legal right to build and worship freely after more than two decades of obstruction.

events.title: Released Pastor Barred from Ministry and Housing After Nine Years in Chinese Prison
events.body:
A pastor in eastern China who served nearly a decade in prison has been released but remains under tight state monitoring and is barred from returning to ministry, according to ChinaAid. The woman, who had led a large congregation for more than thirty years, was released in 2025 after serving nine years of a twelve-year sentence, having received a two-year reduction. Despite her release, she has been prohibited from preaching and from living at the church that was once her home.

The couple's imprisonment began after they refused to comply with a regional government campaign in 2015 that forcibly removed crosses from hundreds of church rooftops. Authorities arrested the pastor, her husband, and several coworkers, and a court sentenced them in 2016 on charges widely regarded as political punishment. Her husband remains imprisoned and is not expected to be released for several more years. State-approved leaders took over their congregation during their imprisonment, and the current leadership has barred the released pastor from both the pulpit and housing. She also faces a court-imposed fine of approximately USD $200,000 with no income or social security benefits. ChinaAid reports that believers attempting to visit or support her face warnings and heightened scrutiny from local authorities.

Pray: May God provide this faithful pastor with safe housing, daily provision, and the lifting of all unjust restrictions on her freedom and ministry.
events.prayer: May God provide this faithful pastor with safe housing, daily provision, and the lifting of all unjust restrictions on her freedom and ministry.

events.title: Christian Prayer Leader Detained in China for Leading Online Prayer Ministry
events.body:
A Christian prayer leader in China was detained on May 9, 2026, after authorities accused her of involvement in illegal religious activities connected to an online prayer ministry linked to a prominent house church, according to ChinaAid. Police placed the woman under administrative detention — a mechanism that allows Chinese authorities to imprison individuals for short periods without a formal criminal trial. She was sentenced to 12 days of detention, running through May 21, 2026.

Authorities accused the woman of "organizing, instigating, coercing, inducing or inciting another person to engage in cult activities, superstitious sects, or secret societies" — charges that rights advocates say are routinely used to suppress unregistered religious activity. The online prayer network she helped lead holds daily prayer meetings and was founded by a house church that has faced years of government pressure, as previously reported by ChinaAid. Chinese authorities have intensified pressure in recent years on unregistered Protestant congregations and independent prayer groups operating outside state-approved religious organizations, and supporters say this latest detention has heightened concerns about religious freedom conditions in the country.

Pray: Lord, grant this detained prayer leader peace and good health during her imprisonment, and protect the online prayer ministry from being silenced.
events.prayer: Lord, grant this detained prayer leader peace and good health during her imprisonment, and protect the online prayer ministry from being silenced.

events.title: Wives of Detained House Church Leaders Face Harassment After Appeal to U.S. President
events.body:
According to ChinaAid, the wives of three detained house church leaders in China issued an open letter to U.S. President Donald Trump in May 2026, coinciding with his visit to China, urging him or his representatives to attend an upcoming court hearing for one of the detained pastors. Shortly after the letter's publication, unidentified individuals began appearing outside the home of at least one of the wives, filming with their phones and repeatedly knocking on her door.

The three church leaders — two pastors and an elder — have been held in detention since March 2024, charged with "organizing illegal gatherings" for activities including officiating Christian weddings and welcoming released believers. Their families maintain the charges are politically motivated. The men belong to a house church, an independent Protestant congregation operating outside China's state-sanctioned religious system. As the case approaches trial, the wives report heightened surveillance and significant psychological pressure, and are appealing to international human rights organizations to monitor the fairness of the judicial proceedings and to call for an end to the harassment of family members.

Pray: Lord, grant courage and protection to the wives and families of these detained pastors, and bring justice and freedom for those imprisoned for their faith in China.
events.prayer: Lord, grant courage and protection to the wives and families of these detained pastors, and bring justice and freedom for those imprisoned for their faith in China.

events.title: Coptic Scholar's Prison Sentence Sends Chilling Warning to Egypt's Christian Community
events.body:
An Egyptian Coptic scholar and online Christian educator has been sentenced to five years in prison with hard labor after being convicted of "misuse of social media" and "contempt of religion," according to Crux Now. The man had been using a YouTube channel with over 100,000 subscribers to teach and defend the Christian faith — activity Egyptian authorities say violates the country's blasphemy laws.

According to Crux Now, an advocate for global religious freedom at ADF International warns the sentence has created a "chilling effect" on Coptic Christians, particularly younger believers and online content creators who engage in apologetics or religious discussion. The advocate described the punishment as appearing deliberately intended to send a broader warning beyond the individual convicted, effectively signaling that nonviolent religious speech can carry severe criminal consequences. In a related development, a court also declined to rule on a petition to establish Easter as a national holiday. Religious freedom advocates say both developments reflect a broader and increasingly coordinated erosion of protections for Egypt's Christian community, raising serious concerns about unequal treatment and the shrinking space for public Christian expression.

Pray: Lord, grant courage and protection to Coptic Christians in Egypt facing growing legal restrictions on their faith, and may justice and religious freedom be restored.
events.prayer: Lord, grant courage and protection to Coptic Christians in Egypt facing growing legal restrictions on their faith, and may justice and religious freedom be restored.

events.title: Muslim Relatives Ambush and Seriously Injure Christian Convert in Uganda
events.body:
A recent convert to Christianity in Uganda was lured into an ambush and beaten unconscious by Muslim relatives after they learned he had left Islam, according to Morning Star News. On May 2, the man — a 28-year-old father — received a phone call from a family member claiming his child was critically ill. Believing the emergency to be genuine, he traveled to his in-laws' home accompanied by his pastor. Upon arrival, he was told to enter the house alone while his pastor waited outside, and was then confronted by multiple relatives who attacked him with fists and sticks, leaving him unconscious.

The man is currently hospitalized with serious head injuries. According to Morning Star News, he had placed his faith in Christ on New Year's Eve at a prayer gathering. Following his conversion, his wife took their child and returned to live with her family. His local church is weighing the risks of reporting the attack to police. Morning Star News notes that Uganda's constitution guarantees religious freedom and the right to convert, but documents many cases where converts from Islam face violent reprisals — particularly in eastern regions of the country where Muslim populations are more concentrated.

Pray: May this convert receive full physical healing, be reunited with his child, and find courage and wisdom as he determines his next steps in faith.
events.prayer: May this convert receive full physical healing, be reunited with his child, and find courage and wisdom as he determines his next steps in faith.

events.title: Chinese Authorities Block House Church Gatherings in Escalating Crackdown
events.body:
Chinese authorities have once again disrupted worship gatherings of a prominent unregistered house church in southwest China, according to ChinaAid. On a Sunday morning in mid-May 2026, police officers blocked access to a planned joint worship gathering of several cell groups. A woman arriving at the venue was stopped by plainclothes officers, who conducted identity registration and issued verbal threats against her.

The incident follows a similar operation approximately two weeks earlier, when officers dispersed two small groups affiliated with the same church, preventing members from holding their regular Sunday service. In response, the groups had planned to consolidate into a joint gathering — but police intervention forced organizers to cancel entirely.

According to ChinaAid, the church has operated under a decentralised, small-group model since a sweeping government raid in December 2018, during which dozens of members were detained. The church's founding pastor was subsequently sentenced to nine years in prison, and four senior church leaders arrested earlier this year remain in custody.

Despite mounting pressure — including home visits, detentions for questioning, and repeated venue blockades — members say they will not abandon their faith. Sources indicate the groups are actively seeking an undisclosed alternative location to continue Sunday worship.

Pray that these believers would find courage and safety as they seek to gather in Christ's name, and that those imprisoned for their faith would be sustained and released.

events.title: USCIRF Warns Syria's Religious Minorities Face Ongoing Sectarian Violence and Extrajudicial Killings
events.body:
Syria's transitional authorities have failed to protect religious minorities from sectarian violence, according to a new report from the U.S. Commission on International Religious Freedom (USCIRF). The report details how both state and nonstate actors have carried out acts of violence — including extrajudicial killings — targeting Alawis, Druze, Christians, and other religious communities across the country.

The Islamic State group continues to pose a severe threat to freedom of religion or belief, attacking houses of worship and killing worshipers. USCIRF's findings indicate that the current governing authorities have been unable or unwilling to establish rule of law sufficient to shield vulnerable religious populations from such violence.

In its 2026 Annual Report, USCIRF recommended that the U.S. Department of State formally designate Syria as a Country of Particular Concern — a classification reserved for nations committing particularly severe violations of religious freedom. The commission, an independent bipartisan agency of the U.S. Congress, monitors religious freedom conditions globally and advises the President, the Secretary of State, and Congress on related foreign policy measures.

The situation reflects a deepening crisis for Syria's Christian community and other minorities, who face threats from multiple armed factions with little institutional protection.

Pray that God would raise up protectors for Syria's vulnerable religious minorities and that His peace would break through the sectarian violence tearing communities apart.

events.title: Woman Imprisoned Over Two Decades in North Korea for Uttering the Word "God"
events.body:
A Christian woman has remained imprisoned in North Korea since 2004, according to the United States Commission on International Religious Freedom (USCIRF), which lists her case in its Freedom of Religion or Belief Victims Database. The woman was detained by Ministry of State Security officials after she was found to have had contact with a religious person and uttered the word "God" — offences considered serious violations under North Korea's totalitarian system. She was initially confined in a state security detention facility before being transferred to a political prison camp, where she remains held to this day with no record of formal charges, conviction, or sentencing. USCIRF confirms she has not been released and has not died in custody, meaning she has endured more than two decades of imprisonment for the most minimal expression of religious identity. Her case illustrates the extreme measures North Korean authorities employ to eradicate any contact with Christianity, where even a single spoken reference to God can result in indefinite detention in one of the country's brutal political prison camps. The international community has largely been unable to intervene in such cases due to North Korea's near-total isolation.

May God sustain this woman with His presence and bring about her freedom and full restoration.

events.title: Christian Woman Remains Held in North Korean Political Prison Camp After More Than a Decade
events.body:
A Christian woman detained in North Korea since 2004 remains imprisoned with no known release, according to the United States Commission on International Religious Freedom (USCIRF). The woman was arrested in 2011 by Ministry of State Security officials and subsequently sentenced to a political prison camp solely on account of her religious activity. USCIRF's Freedom of Religion or Belief Victims Database lists her current status as "not released," meaning she has now endured more than a decade of imprisonment in one of the world's most brutal penal systems. North Korea's political prison camps, known for extreme conditions and severe human rights abuses, are frequently used to punish citizens found engaging in Christian practice, which the regime treats as a grave political threat. No charges, conviction details, or sentencing specifics have been made publicly available in her case. USCIRF continues to document her case as part of broader efforts to hold the North Korean government accountable for systematic religious persecution. Her case reflects the wider pattern of Christians being subjected to arbitrary detention and long-term imprisonment simply for practicing their faith in the country.

May God sustain this woman with His presence in the darkness of captivity and move the hearts of those with influence to secure her freedom.

events.title: Christian Man Held Incommunicado in North Korea for Over a Decade, USCIRF Reports
events.body:
A Christian man in North Korea has remained detained since 2014 with no indication of release, according to the U.S. Commission on International Religious Freedom (USCIRF). The man was arrested by Ministry of State Security officials for practicing his Christian faith and engaging in evangelism. Following his arrest, he was confined at a state security detention facility and held incommunicado, meaning no contact with family or outside parties has been permitted.

USCIRF's Freedom of Religion or Belief Victims Database lists the man as not released, with no recorded dates for formal charges, conviction, or sentencing — a pattern consistent with North Korea's opaque and arbitrary detention system. He is confirmed to still be alive in custody, though his current condition remains unknown.

North Korea consistently ranks among the worst persecutors of Christians in the world, where the practice of Christianity outside state-approved structures is treated as a serious political and ideological threat. Believers risk arrest, imprisonment in brutal labour camps, and execution. Cases like this one represent only a fraction of the persecution believed to be occurring, as the hermetic nature of the regime makes comprehensive documentation nearly impossible.

Pray for this man's safety, for his family who have had no contact with him, and for an end to the systematic crushing of faith in North Korea.

events.title: Christian Man Sentenced to Indefinite Detention in North Korea for Smuggling Bible Pages
events.body:
A Christian man has been imprisoned in North Korea since 2008 with no known release, according to the United States Commission on International Religious Freedom (USCIRF). The man was arrested by Ministry of State Security officials after being caught smuggling pages of the Bible into the country from a church in China. Following extensive interrogation by authorities, he was sentenced to indefinite imprisonment at a political prison camp. USCIRF records confirm he remains detained and has not died in custody, though his precise current condition is unknown. His case highlights the extreme danger faced by Christians in North Korea, where possession of religious materials is treated as a serious criminal offense by the state. The regime systematically targets citizens found engaging in any unauthorized religious activity, particularly those with connections to Christian communities abroad. Cross-border religious networks, through which Bibles and other materials are passed into the country, are actively monitored and suppressed by state security services. North Korea consistently ranks among the most dangerous countries in the world for Christians, with believers facing arrest, torture, forced labor, and indefinite detention. This case represents one of many documented instances of severe religious persecution recorded in USCIRF's Freedom of Religion or Belief victims database.

Pray for this man held in indefinite detention, that God would sustain him, bring him comfort, and move the hearts of those who hold authority over his life.

events.title: North Korean Soldier and Family Imprisoned After Bible Smuggled Into Country
events.body:
A male soldier serving in the Korean People's Army was arrested on an unspecified date after authorities discovered he had smuggled a Bible into North Korea, according to the USCIRF Freedom of Religion or Belief Victims Database. Security forces from the Korean People's Army and the Ministry of State Security carried out the arrest. Following his detention, one of his parents was executed, while the soldier himself and his remaining family members were imprisoned indefinitely at a political prison camp. He has not been released and remains imprisoned for his religious activity. No dates of charge, conviction, or sentencing have been recorded. The case illustrates the extreme consequences faced by Christians in North Korea, where possession of religious literature is treated as a serious political crime punishable not only for the individual but also for their entire family. North Korea continues to be regarded as one of the most dangerous countries in the world for Christians, with the state exercising total control over all religious expression. The fate of the soldier and his family members remains unknown.

Pray for this soldier and his family, that God would sustain them with hope and strength in the darkness of imprisonment, and that doors of mercy would open for their release.

events.title: ADF Kills Dozens in Targeted Attacks on Christians in Eastern DR Congo
events.body:
ADF rebels affiliated with the so-called Islamic State have carried out a series of devastating attacks against civilians in eastern DR Congo, killing at least 44 people over two days in a Christian-majority region, according to International Christian Concern. A civil society organization reported that rebels struck multiple rural areas between May 5 and May 6, killing 24 civilians, followed by a massacre of 20 more people in broad daylight on May 7.

The attacks drew condemnation from Washington, with a senior U.S. State Department advisor for Africa stating that the violence constitutes targeted aggression against Christian communities and reaffirming American commitment to peace in the Great Lakes region. The official expressed solidarity with persecuted Christians and called the threat urgent.

The ADF has waged a decade-long campaign of terror in eastern DR Congo, including killings, abductions, forced labor, and the recruitment of children. A recent Amnesty International report documented widespread war crimes by the group, with women and girls particularly targeted. Joint military operations by Congolese and Ugandan forces since 2021 have failed to curb the violence. Legislators in the capital have called for greater international attention to the ADF threat, comparable to diplomatic efforts addressing other armed conflicts in the country. Displaced civilians fleeing to larger cities now face hunger and insecurity.

May believers in eastern DR Congo find protection, provision, and perseverance as they endure relentless terror.

events.title: ADF Kills Nine in Eastern DR Congo Attack Days After Massacre of Over Twenty Civilians
events.body:
Armed fighters from the Allied Democratic Forces (ADF) attacked a civilian area in a province of eastern DR Congo on May 10, killing at least nine people with machetes, burning several homes, and forcing residents to flee under cover of darkness, according to International Christian Concern. The assault came just three days after a separate ADF attack left more than 26 civilians dead and others kidnapped in a nearby area — an incident where residents initially mistook the rebels for national army soldiers, making escape nearly impossible.

Local sources report that attackers entered the settlement around 7:30 p.m. Bodies were transported to a nearby health facility, and military authorities had not officially commented at the time of reporting. The attacks are part of a broader pattern of violence this month, with multiple villages struck in rapid succession.

A community leader described the devastation: "In our villages, it is no longer water that flows, it is blood." A regional parliamentarian condemned the lack of security response, noting that warnings about rebel presence near the area had been issued before the attack, yet the population was left unprotected.

Analysts observing the region suggest the ADF is pursuing a deliberate strategy of rapid, repeated strikes on populated areas to trigger mass displacement and sustain a climate of terror among the Christian-majority civilian population.

Pray for God's protection over believers in eastern DR Congo and for justice to reach those perpetrating these brutal attacks.

events.title: ADF Rebels Kill 21 Christians in Nighttime Attack in Eastern DRC
events.body:
At least 21 Christians were killed when Allied Democratic Forces rebels launched a nighttime attack on a village in an eastern province of the Democratic Republic of Congo on May 13, according to International Christian Concern. Homes were burned down as armed rebels entered the community, tied residents, and killed believers with large bladed weapons while victims cried out to Jesus Christ. One survivor reported hiding under a bed while listening to the sounds of the slaughter and cries for help.

The attack is part of a series of assaults beginning May 5 that have spread to surrounding communities, with locals describing ongoing fear and uncertainty each night. Survivors have described discovering additional victims days after attacks, sometimes only locating bodies by smell. The Islamic militant group is widely reported to use lethal violence as a means of religious expansion throughout eastern Congo.

Community leaders have publicly condemned the killings, calling on religious, cultural, and political leaders to unite in response. According to International Christian Concern, the area has become one of the deadliest flashpoints for anti-Christian violence in the region, with residents describing a climate of fear in which no one knows whether they will survive the night.

Pray for surviving families in eastern Congo to find refuge, comfort, and protection as violence continues to threaten their communities.

events.title: Six Chinese Christians Arrested for Running Children's Sunday School Classes
events.body:
Authorities in a southwestern province of China have arrested six Christians from a house church on charges including "fraud" and the unprecedented accusation of "organizing minors to engage in activities undermining public order," according to ChinaAid. The arrests targeted five men and one woman, all affiliated with the same congregation.

Sources familiar with the case confirmed to ChinaAid that the charges against the believers stem primarily from ordinary church activities, including conducting Sunday school classes and facilitating family participation in Christian worship. Legal experts within China have raised serious concerns about both the substance of the charges and procedural violations, including reports that the local procuratorate approved the arrests without properly hearing attorneys' legal opinions as required by law.

Religious freedom advocates describe the application of public order statutes — traditionally reserved for conduct such as fighting or theft — to religious education as a significant and alarming expansion of criminal law. ChinaAid notes that authorities in the same area previously sentenced a religious leader to 12 years in prison on fraud-related charges, suggesting a pattern of using criminal statutes to suppress independent religious practice.

The case has drawn widespread concern among observers, who warn it represents a troubling escalation in China's broader campaign against unregistered Christian communities, particularly regarding the rights of parents to raise their children in the faith.

Pray that God would grant courage and peace to the detained believers and their families, and that justice would prevail.

events.title: Chinese House Church Pastor Held Over 300 Days Without Verdict as Family Calls for Release
events.body:
A Protestant pastor in eastern China has been held without verdict for more than 300 days following his arrest in July 2025, according to ChinaAid. The pastor, who leads an unregistered house church, was detained by police in a cross-provincial operation and subsequently transferred to authorities in his home region, where he remains in custody.

On May 7, his wife and young daughter briefly glimpsed him through the window of a police vehicle as he was transported from a pretrial court conference. Despite months of detention, he smiled at his family through the glass. As the vehicle departed, his wife made a heart gesture toward him, and their daughter called out, "Daddy, stay strong!" His wife later described the fleeting reunion as deeply meaningful after nearly ten months apart.

ChinaAid reports that the house church has faced sustained harassment since 2021, including surveillance of congregants, pressure on members to relocate, and approximately 20 detentions among its membership. In an earlier incident, the pastor was questioned for praying with believers outside a detention facility, with police presenting photographs of his prayers as evidence. When he asked whether prayer itself was a crime, officers reportedly responded that the prayer was unregistered.

ChinaAid states the case reflects wider pressure on unregistered Protestant congregations operating outside state-approved religious structures in China. No verdict has been issued.

Pray for this pastor and his family to know God's sustaining peace, and for his release.

events.title: Hundreds of Christian Girls Kidnapped and Forcibly Married in Pakistan Each Year, ICC Reports
events.body:
Each year in Pakistan, an estimated 1,000 young girls — many of them Christian — are kidnapped, forcibly converted to Islam, and married against their will to older Muslim men, according to International Christian Concern (ICC). When families attempt to reclaim their daughters through the courts, they frequently face insurmountable obstacles: without birth certificates proving a girl's age, judges often rule in favour of the accused, permanently separating children from their parents.

ICC has documented multiple ongoing cases that illustrate the systemic nature of this abuse. In one case, a Christian girl was abducted at gunpoint after rejecting a marriage proposal. Though a higher court eventually ordered her return following evidence of coercion, her family continues to face violent threats and has been forced to relocate repeatedly. In another case, Pakistan's Federal Constitutional Court ruled that a girl — reportedly around 13 years old at the time of her abduction — was of "mature age," validating her forced conversion and marriage under Islamic law. In a third case, a 12-year-old girl was discovered chained in a cattle pen at the home of a 45-year-old man, six months after her disappearance.

ICC is providing affected families with safe shelter, legal support, and trauma care to help break this cycle of exploitation. The organisation continues to advocate on behalf of persecuted Christian girls and their families.

Pray for Pakistan's Christian girls living in fear, that God would grant them protection, justice, and healing.

events.title: At Least 34 Killed as Fulani Militants Strike Nigerian Christian Communities, Rare Resistance Repels Some Attacks
events.body:
Fulani militant attacks on Christian communities in Nigeria left at least 34 people dead last week, even as rare community resistance partially repelled several assaults, according to Morning Star News.

In one state, attackers struck twice within days, killing three people and burning homes and farms in one incident, then killing five more in a separate attack days later. Combined with violence elsewhere, at least 33 civilians and one police officer were killed, with 21 others wounded.

In a more encouraging development, advance intelligence allowed residents and vigilantes in several rural communities to coordinate a response that forced armed militants to retreat before attacks could be carried out, according to Morning Star News, which cited Truth Nigeria.

In another state, hundreds of gunmen on motorcycles surrounded a town and opened fire, intending to raid it. Approximately 60 volunteers armed with locally made shotguns and 40 police officers prevented a full breach of the perimeter. Nevertheless, eight people including a police inspector were killed and eleven wounded before residents fled their homes.

A police source told Truth Nigeria that over 100 local officers had received training but lacked firearms, while community defenders who arm themselves risk arrest. A representative from an international advocacy body noted that local defenders remain dangerously outgunned.
